summary: Tested the conversation delete/end feature (iteration 16). All 10 specified features verified working correctly via both backend API tests (8/8 pass) and full UI E2E tests with studio and customer accounts.

minor.issue: When a new message is sent to a conversation where both parties have deleted (deleted_by has both user IDs), the deleted_by flags are NOT reset. The conversation remains inaccessible to both parties even after a new message is sent. The send_message endpoint should unset/clear deleted_by on new message to allow both parties to see it again.

action_items: Minor: POST /api/messages should unset `deleted_by` when a new message is sent to a previously-fully-deleted conversation, so both parties can see it again. Currently, once both parties delete, new messages don't restore visibility.
critical_code_review_comments: isEndedByOther logic (line 298 MessagesPage.js): `Object.keys(activeConvData.deleted_by).length > 0` is correct because GET /messages already filters out convs where current user deleted - so any deleted_by entries must be from the other party
DELETE endpoint correctly: creates system message with is_system:true, sets deleted_by.{user_id}, updates last_message
GET /messages filter uses MongoDB dot notation `deleted_by.{user_id}: {$exists: False}` - correct
2.5s conversation polling (pollConvRef) correctly picks up deleted_by changes without page refresh
send_message does not reset deleted_by - see minor backend issue above
seed_data_creation: Used DB reset (cleared deleted_by) to enable UI testing. Both parties have fully deleted the test conversation at end of testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All delete/end conversation features verified. The studio (test_studio) and customer (test_customer) conversation has been fully deleted by both parties. To restore for future tests: clear deleted_by field in MongoDB for conv_id 'conv_69de3148403e3dba6fa5c434_69de3148403e3dba6fa5c435'. Known minor issue: send_message doesn't reset deleted_by flags when new message sent to fully-deleted conv.
